revenue_by_merchant.sql:
SELECT m.merchant_name,
       SUM(t.amount) as total_revenue,
       COUNT(*) as txn_count
FROM fact_transactions t, dim_merchant m
WHERE t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
AND t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01'
GROUP BY m.merchant_name
ORDER BY total_revenue
LIMIT 10;

issues.title: Incorrect revenue calculation
issues.problem: Revenue should only include completed transactions, but the query sums all transaction amounts.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as total_revenue

issues.title: Potential performance issue
issues.problem: Using implicit join syntax can lead to less readable and potentially less performant queries. It's better to use explicit JOIN syntax.
issues.fix: FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id

issues.title: Missing comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining its purpose and logic, which can be helpful for future maintainers.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose of each part of the query.

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT m.merchant_name,
       SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as total_revenue,
       COUNT(*) as txn_count
FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
WHERE t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01'
GROUP BY m.merchant_name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 10;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with revenue calculation and a readability issue with missing comments. It also used implicit join syntax which can be less performant.

customer_spend.sql:
SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier,
       SUM(t.amount) as lifetime_value,
       COUNT(t.transaction_id) as total_orders
FROM dim_customer c
LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id
WHERE t.status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email
HAVING SUM(t.amount) > 1000
ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC;

issues.title: Incorrect SUM calculation
issues.problem: The SUM function is not correctly filtering for 'COMPLETED' status.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value

issues.title: Missing filter for 'COMPLETED' status
issues.problem: The WHERE clause should be in the JOIN condition to filter correctly.
issues.fix: LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id AND t.status = 'COMPLETED'

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic and calculations.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose of each part of the query.

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier,
       SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value,
       COUNT(t.transaction_id) as total_orders
FROM dim_customer c
LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id AND t.status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email
HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) > 1000
ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC;
review.summary: The query had issues with the calculation of lifetime value and missing filters. Readability was also lacking due to the absence of comments.

daily_failure_rate.sql:
SELECT transaction_date,
       (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fact_transactions f2
        WHERE f2.status = 'FAILED'
        AND f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date) as failed_count,
       COUNT(*) as total_count,
       failed_count / total_count * 100 as failure_rate
FROM fact_transactions t
GROUP BY transaction_date
ORDER BY transaction_date;

issues.title: Incorrect calculation of failure rate
issues.problem: The failure rate is being calculated incorrectly by dividing the count of failed transactions by the total count of transactions per day, which is not the correct formula.

issues.title: Correlated subquery
issues.line: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fact_transactions f2 WHERE f2.status = 'FAILED' AND f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date
issues.problem: The use of a correlated subquery can lead to performance issues as it will be executed for each row in the outer query.
issues.fix: Use a JOIN with a pre-aggregated subquery.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, which makes it harder to understand for other developers.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose of each part of the query.

review.corrected_sql: WITH failed_transactions AS (SELECT transaction_date, COUNT(*) as failed_count FROM fact_transactions WHERE status = 'FAILED' GROUP BY transaction_date), total_transactions AS (SELECT transaction_date, COUNT(*) as total_count FROM fact_transactions GROUP BY transaction_date) SELECT t.transaction_date, ft.failed_count, tt.total_count, ft.failed_count * 100.0 / tt.total_count as failure_rate FROM total_transactions tt JOIN failed_transactions ft ON tt.transaction_date = ft.transaction_date ORDER BY t.transaction_date;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with the failure rate calculation, a performance issue with the correlated subquery, and a readability issue with the lack of comments.
